summary: P1 retest iteration 4. Backend: 82/82 pytest pass (test_p1.py 27/27, test_auth.py 16/16, test_content.py 39/39) — all three iteration-3 backend bugs are fixed (brute-force lockout returns 429 with 'Try again in N minute(s)', 2FA /status returns backup_codes_remaining=10, email-only lockout key works behind ingress). Frontend: 2FA setup dialog now correctly renders the QR + secret + verify input. Discovered one NEW frontend bug while doing the full 2FA login E2E: AuthContext.jsx exposed login/register/logout but NOT verify2fa in the provider value, so Login.jsx threw `TypeError: verify2fa is not a function` at the 2FA challenge step. Applied a one-line fix (added verify2fa to the context value). After that, the full 2FA flow (enable → save backup codes → logout → login with TOTP → disable → login without 2FA) passes end-to-end. TipTap editor E2E on /research and PDF upload E2E on /reports both PASS — detail-body HTML contains <strong> and <h2>, report-pdf-block and report-pdf-open are visible. Admin restored to baseline (totp_enabled=false, totp_secret=null).

minor.issue: Non-verified sender domain causes Resend to reject most recipients; publish endpoints still return 200 because dispatch runs in BackgroundTasks. Operationally noisy in logs but matches the iteration-3 note that this is expected for the test environment.

ui_bugs.issue: verify2fa function was defined inside the AuthProvider but NOT exposed in the Provider's value object (line 76). Net effect: clicking the 2FA verify button on the login page raised `TypeError: verify2fa is not a function` and blocked the entire 2FA login flow. Fix applied (testing agent): added verify2fa to the value object. Verified end-to-end after fix.

action_items: Already fixed by testing agent: AuthContext.jsx now exposes verify2fa. Main agent should review this one-line change and confirm no further auth context refactor is desired.
Operational (non-blocking): switch Resend `from` to a verified domain so digest emails deliver to non-owner addresses.
Code quality reminder from iteration 3 still standing: server.py at ~1183 lines should be split into auth/content/uploads/2fa modules.
critical_code_review_comments: Whenever a new AuthContext helper is added (e.g., verify2fa), it must also be listed in the Provider's value object — consider an explicit `value = { user, login, register, logout, verify2fa }` pattern with a type/PropTypes shape so the omission is caught at build/lint time.
ensure_utc() helper added in server.py is a clean fix. Consider applying the same coercion in any other place that compares Mongo-returned datetimes (search for `> now_utc()` / `< now_utc()` calls).
Email-only lockout key is correct behind ingress; consider also a per-IP soft counter if you ever want IP-based rate limiting in addition.
server.py is still ~1183 LOC; split urgently.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on 2FA enable → login → disable, TipTap rich-text save+render, PDF upload + report detail rendering (after the one-line AuthContext fix)

test_credentials: admin@hamptoncrest.com / Hampton#2026 — confirmed working at end of run with totp_enabled=false, totp_secret=null. Safe for future iterations.
seed_data_creation: Test artifacts from this run: a couple of TEST_TipTap_E2E research notes and TEST_Report_PDF_E2E reports; pytest creates and deletes its own TEST_P1_* docs. login_attempts collection cleared at end of run. Multiple bf_user_* / bf_ok_* / test_p1_* users remain in users collection from pytest brute-force and 2FA suites (intentional — they expire/age out).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All P1 scope verified. The verify2fa AuthContext fix is required for the 2FA login UI to work — main agent should keep that change. Pytest suite is now a clean 82/82 regression baseline. For any future auth changes, ensure new context helpers are added to the Provider's value object. If lockout tests are ever flaky, clear `db.login_attempts.delete_many({})` before running.
rca_of_the_issue: Frontend `verify2fa is not a function` — RCA: AuthContext.jsx line 76 destructured context value `{user, login, register, logout}` did not include the locally-defined `verify2fa` function, so the consumer `Login.jsx` (line 9 `const { ... verify2fa } = useAuth();`) saw it as undefined. Reproduction: enable 2FA on any account, log out, log back in, enter TOTP at the challenge step → onSubmit calls verify2fa(...) → TypeError. Mitigation applied: added `verify2fa` to the Provider's `value` object so it is exported to consumers. After-fix verification: full 2FA login flow passes including dashboard navigation.
